style: Art Nouveau / Eclectic with American high-rise influence

key_features_for_minecraft.ground_floor: Natural stone (hardstone) arcade base with shops/cafe spaces, columns, floral motifs, winged dragon reliefs at base of corner towers
key_features_for_minecraft.first_floor: Also natural stone cladding, 6 sculptures by Simon Miedema (Navigation, Progress, Agriculture, Industry, Trade, and one more)
key_features_for_minecraft.floors_2_to_7: White glazed brick facade with geometric decorations in yellow, red, and blue brick; colorful tile panels with Art Nouveau floral motifs in arches crowning window rows
key_features_for_minecraft.corner_towers: Two corner towers with fairytale turret silhouettes
key_features_for_minecraft.roof: Flat slate pitched roof with ornate roofline, wrought-iron elements
key_features_for_minecraft.windows: Rounded corner windows with stone reliefs
key_features_for_minecraft.other: Wrought-iron lamp hooks with floral motifs

image_sources.description: Full exterior view of Witte Huis from street level, showing complete facade and corner towers
image_sources.useful_for: Overall building shape, proportions, tower placement, white facade color

image_sources.description: Witte Huis exterior view showing full building with surrounding context at Oudehaven

image_sources.useful_for: Building proportions relative to surroundings, roof profile, overall massing

image_sources.description: Another angle of Witte Huis exterior
image_sources.credit: Wikimedia Commons - Wiki Loves Monuments 2013 (CC license)
image_sources.useful_for: Alternative angle showing facade details and corner tower profile

image_sources.description: Detail of 'Handel' (Trade) sculpture on the Witte Huis facade by Simon Miedema
image_sources.useful_for: Art Nouveau sculptural detail at first floor level, decorative elements

image_sources.description: Witte Huis full view from across the harbor, showing the building in its waterfront context (2010 photo)
image_sources.useful_for: Full building view from distance showing complete silhouette including mansard roof and turrets

image_sources.description: Witte Huis on Google Arts & Culture - high quality archival/artistic image by Willem Molenbroek
image_sources.useful_for: High quality reference image, possibly historical photograph or rendering by the architect

image_sources.description: Bluffton University architectural photography page - multiple detailed views by Mary Ann Sullivan
image_sources.useful_for: Multiple detailed architectural photos: facade details, ground floor arcade, decorative elements, corner towers

image_sources.description: Francis Frith historic photograph of Witte Huis c.1930
image_sources.useful_for: Historic view of the building c.1930, shows original context before WWII bombing of surrounding area

image_sources.description: Accidentally Wes Anderson feature on Witte Huis - stylized architectural photograph
image_sources.useful_for: Clean, well-composed exterior photo emphasizing symmetry and color palette

image_sources.description: Alamy stock photo showing Art Nouveau facade details
image_sources.useful_for: Professional architectural photography showing Art Nouveau style details

image_sources.description: Rotterdam360 comprehensive photo gallery of Witte Huis
image_sources.useful_for: Multiple views and details of the building, local Rotterdam perspective

image_sources.description: Structurae engineering database entry with technical building data
image_sources.useful_for: Technical/engineering perspective, structural details useful for understanding construction

architectural_drawings.description: Historic design/architectural rendering by architect Willem Molenbroek (photographer Van den Ende, Stadsarchief Collectie 4202, nummer 1167)
architectural_drawings.notes: Rotterdam City Archive holds the original architectural design photos. Search their image bank for Collectie 4202, nummer 1167.
Shows building under construction, useful for understanding structural layers. Search Stadsarchief image bank.

architectural_drawings.description: Rijksmonumenten.nl monument description with facade details breakdown
architectural_drawings.notes: Official Dutch heritage listing. Contains detailed written description of all facade elements floor by floor, useful as a textual blueprint.
Additional heritage documentation and images of the monument.

minecraft_build_notes.scale_suggestion: At 43m tall with 10 floors, consider 1:1 scale (43 blocks tall) or 2:1 (86 blocks) for detail

palette_suggestions.white_glazed_facade: White concrete, white terracotta, or quartz blocks
palette_suggestions.ground_floor_stone: Stone bricks or polished andesite for natural stone arcade
palette_suggestions.colored_decorations: Yellow terracotta, blue terracotta, red terracotta for geometric accents
palette_suggestions.roof_slate: Dark gray or cyan terracotta, deepslate tiles
palette_suggestions.art_nouveau_panels: Glazed terracotta blocks in various colors for decorative panels
palette_suggestions.corner_tower_tops: Use stairs and slabs for turret silhouettes

minecraft_build_notes.key_proportions: Almost square ground plan, 10 floors, two prominent corner towers, flat pitched roof
distinctive_elements: White glazed brick exterior (100,000 bricks in reality)
Two corner towers with fairytale turret tops
Natural stone ground floor with arcade columns
Winged dragon reliefs at base of corner towers
Art Nouveau mosaic panels in window arches
Text 'Het Witte Huis' on east facade
Geometric colored brick patterns (yellow, red, blue) on white field
